chunks.title: Instructor Office Hours
chunks.content: Garcia (Wed 1-2 pm at https://tamu.zoom.us/j/98802916283) or by appointment

chunks.title: Description
chunks.content: This course introduces analytical and numerical tools for solving stochastic and deterministic dynamic optimization problems in discrete time. The course is centered around a Markov decision process (MDP) formulation of stochastic dynamic optimization. Bellman's equation and properties of the value function and of the optimal policy function are discussed for finite and infinite horizon problems. The course closes with a survey of reinforcement learning methods such as Q-Learning, policy gradient and actor-critic.

chunks.title: Objectives
chunks.content:
After taking this course, students will have the ability to:
1. Ability to formulate and analyze stochastic dynamic optimization problems.
2. Ability to use of numerical and/or statistical techniques to identify optimal solutions.

chunks.title: Assignments & Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Homework/Exercises (40%) 1 Mid-term Exam (25%) Final Project (35%)
All assignments are expected to be completed individually unless otherwise specified. Any disagreements regarding a grade received on any graded material must be discussed within one week of the return of the graded material. No grade will be changed beyond the one week limit. If you request a regrade, the entire assignment will be regarded (i.e. if we made a mistake in your favor, this will also be corrected). Grades will be calculated on the basis of total points earned. If deemed necessary, the grade thresholds may be curved at the instructor's discretion. The thresholds for letter grades are:
A: 90-100%
B: 80-89%
C: 70-79%
D: 60-69%
F: 59% and lower

chunks.title: Course Attendance Policy
chunks.content: You are expected to attend all class lectures except for university excused absences. The university rule regarding excused absences can be found at http://student-rules.tamu.edu/rule07.

chunks.title: Office Hours Policy
chunks.content: We are usually in our offices during office hours and are glad to discuss anything with you except material covered during an unexcused absence. If other students are already in our offices, please join them. If you would like to discuss a private matter with us please schedule an appointment.

chunks.title: Academic Honesty (Course-Specific Policy)
chunks.content:
In this course, we will request the Aggie Honor Office to assign a grade of F* for any violation of the Aggie Honor Code, no matter how minimal the violation may be. (The Honor Council Rules and Procedures can be found at http://aggiehonor.tamu.edu.)
It is acceptable for you to discuss any assignments except exams with other students prior to the due date. After discussing an assignment with someone else, do not write anything that you do not understand and always use your own language on the assignments. Copying another student's writing directly, even if it is a portion of the assignment, is considered cheating and an Aggie Honor Code violation. No leniency is given for academic dishonesty.

chunks.title: The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)
chunks.content: a federal anti-discrimination statute that provides comprehensive civil rights protection for persons with disabilities. Among other things, this legislation requires that all students with disabilities be guaranteed a learning environment that provides for reasonable accommodation of their disabilities. If you believe you have a disability requiring an accommodation, please contact Disability Services, currently located in the Disability Services building at the Student Services at White Creek complex on west campus or call 979-845-1637. For additional information visit http://disability.tamu.edu.

chunks.title: Statement on Diversity and Inclusion
chunks.content: It is important to us that all students feel accepted and comfortable in this class. If for any reason you feel disrespected, uncomfortable, or unsafe in our classroom please discuss your concerns with us via the medium you are most comfortable with. If you do not feel comfortable communicating directly to us, please reach out to Dr. Ntaimo (department head).

chunks.title: Other Pertinent Course Information
chunks.content: All information related to the course, including handouts, will be made available on ecampus.tamu.edu. All communication related to the course will be sent electronically via eCampus as announcements or delivered to your @tamu.edu mailbox. You are expected to check your email regularly.

chunks.title: Course Outline
chunks.content:
Topic
1 Review of Markov Processes, Ergodic Theory and Stationary Processes.
2 Markov Decision Process (MDP), Examples: Inventory Control, Linear Quadratic Control.
3 Finite Horizon Dynamic Programming, Principle of Optimality, Value Iteration.
4 Infinite Horizon Dynamic Programming. Policy Iteration. Euler's Equation.
5 Partially Observable MDPs.
Midterm
8 Reinforcement Learning.
9 Regularized MDPs, Q-Learning.
10 Policy Gradients.
11 Actor-Critic methods.
12 Inverse Reinforcement Learning.
13 Stochastic Dynamic Games. Markov-Perfect Equilibrium.

warnings: TA name and email not specified
Schedule has no specific dates for topics or assignments, only a list of topics and 'Midterm'
